title: Bridge Essay: Editorial: The Ups and Downs of Mind-Wandering in Adolescents.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Non-fabrication** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Spontaneous thought — mind-wandering, default mode network, creative cognition, involuntary thought.

**Editorial: The Ups and Downs of Mind-Wandering in Adolescents.**. J Am Acad Child Adolesc Psychiatry.. PMID: 32561405. DOI: 10.1016/j.jaac.2020.06.001. The human brain is always active; it wanders freely during rest as well as when we lose focus during tasks. Mind-wandering encompasses spontaneous thinking, such as processing recent experiences, problem solving, and achieving insights. Understanding this unconstrained brain activity may lead to clues about the neural mechanisms of mental health problems.
